enonce.texte: Match the following job interview and personal qualities vocabulary with their correct definitions. This exercise will help you master the key vocabulary needed for job interviews.

questions.texte: Match each term (a-e) with its correct definition (i-v).

sousQuestions.texte: Interview
sousQuestions.reponse: A formal meeting in which a candidate is asked questions to assess their suitability for a job.

sousQuestions.texte: Position
sousQuestions.reponse: A job or role within an organization.

sousQuestions.texte: Qualifications
sousQuestions.reponse: The skills, experience, or education required for a particular job.

sousQuestions.texte: Salary
sousQuestions.reponse: A fixed regular payment, typically paid monthly or weekly, for work.

sousQuestions.texte: Applicant
sousQuestions.reponse: A person who applies for a job.

questions.texte: Fill in the blank with the correct word from the list: [interview, position, qualifications, salary, applicant].
questions.reponse: The applicant carefully prepared for the job interview.

sousQuestions.texte: The _______ carefully prepared for the job _______.
sousQuestions.reponse: applicant, interview

sousQuestions.texte: The _______ requires a degree in business administration.
sousQuestions.reponse: position

sousQuestions.texte: Her impressive _______ made her a strong candidate.
sousQuestions.reponse: qualifications

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences using polite forms (could, would) to ask questions in a job interview context.

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with 'could' or 'would' to make the questions more polite.

sousQuestions.texte: _______ you tell me more about the responsibilities of this position?
sousQuestions.reponse: Could / Would

sousQuestions.texte: _______ I ask about the salary range for this role?
sousQuestions.reponse: Could / May

sousQuestions.texte: _______ you be interested in hearing about my previous experience?
sousQuestions.reponse: Would

sousQuestions.texte: _______ you please explain the career progression opportunities?
sousQuestions.reponse: Could / Would

questions.texte: Rewrite the following direct questions using polite forms.

sousQuestions.texte: What are the working hours?
sousQuestions.reponse: Could you tell me what the working hours are? / Would you mind telling me about the working hours?

sousQuestions.texte: When will I hear back?
sousQuestions.reponse: Could you let me know when I will hear back?

enonce.texte: Match the personal qualities with their correct descriptions. These are qualities employers often look for in job candidates.

questions.texte: Match each quality (a-e) with its description (i-v).

sousQuestions.texte: Hard-working
sousQuestions.reponse: Diligent and putting a lot of effort into work.

sousQuestions.texte: Motivated
sousQuestions.reponse: Having a strong reason or drive to do something.

sousQuestions.texte: Skilled
sousQuestions.reponse: Having the ability to do something well.

sousQuestions.texte: Experienced
sousQuestions.reponse: Having practical knowledge or skill gained through doing something.

sousQuestions.texte: Qualified
sousQuestions.reponse: Having the necessary skills, experience, or education for a particular job.

questions.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct personal quality word.

sousQuestions.texte: She is a _______ worker who always finishes her tasks on time.
sousQuestions.reponse: hard-working

sousQuestions.texte: Our company is looking for _______ candidates who can start immediately.
sousQuestions.reponse: qualified

sousQuestions.texte: He has five years of _______ in the marketing industry.
sousQuestions.reponse: experience

enonce.texte:
Read the following job interview scenario and answer the questions below.

Job Interview Scenario

Marie is applying for a position as a Sales Manager at a technology company. She arrives early for the interview, dressed professionally. The interviewer, Mr. Johnson, greets her warmly and invites her to sit down.

Mr. Johnson: "Could you tell me about your previous experience in sales?"

Marie: "I have worked in sales for five years. At my previous job, I increased sales by 30% in just one year. I am highly motivated and I enjoy working with clients."

Mr. Johnson: "That's impressive. What are your strengths?"

Marie: "I am hard-working, skilled in communication, and I am a team player. I believe these qualities would make me a great addition to your team."

Mr. Johnson: "Would you be available to start next month?"

Marie: "Yes, I would. I'm very excited about this opportunity."

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the scenario.

sousQuestions.texte: What position is Marie applying for?
sousQuestions.reponse: Sales Manager at a technology company.

sousQuestions.texte: What were Marie's achievements at her previous job?
sousQuestions.reponse: She increased sales by 30% in one year.

sousQuestions.texte: What qualities does Marie mention as her strengths?
sousQuestions.reponse: She is hard-working, skilled in communication, and a team player.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify two polite forms used in the interview and explain their purpose.
sousQuestions.reponse:
1. 'Could you tell me...' - used to make a polite request for information.
2. 'Would you be available...' - used to ask politely about availability.

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph describing your strengths and why you would be a good candidate for a job. Use at least two polite forms (could, would) in your writing.

questions.texte: Write a paragraph of about 100 words describing your strengths.
questions.reponse: A well-structured paragraph describing personal qualities and strengths.

sousQuestions.texte: Example: "I believe I would be a great candidate for this position. I am hard-working and motivated. I have strong communication skills and enjoy working in teams. I am also very organized and can manage multiple tasks efficiently. If you were to ask my previous supervisor, he would tell you that I am reliable and dedicated. I would love the opportunity to contribute to your company's success."
sousQuestions.reponse: Uses polite forms (would) and describes personal strengths clearly.

enonce.texte: Work with a partner. One person acts as the interviewer and the other as the candidate. Prepare and conduct a mock job interview. Use polite forms and appropriate vocabulary.

questions.texte: Prepare the following elements for your mock interview:

sousQuestions.texte: Interviewer: Prepare questions about experience, strengths, weaknesses, and availability.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Could you tell me about your previous work experience?', 'What are your greatest strengths?', 'Would you be willing to travel for work?'

sousQuestions.texte: Candidate: Prepare answers about your experience and skills.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'I have three years of experience in customer service. I am hard-working and motivated. I would be happy to start next month.'

sousQuestions.texte: Perform the interview and then switch roles.
sousQuestions.reponse: Successful performance of the mock interview using polite forms and appropriate vocabulary.

intitule: Analyzing a Job Advertisement and Preparing for an Interview

enonce.texte:
Read the following job advertisement and prepare for a mock interview based on it.

Job Advertisement: Administrative Assistant

Company: ABC Business Solutions

Location: Yaoundé, Cameroon

Requirements:

  • Bachelor's degree in Business Administration or related field
  • Excellent communication and organizational skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office
  • At least 2 years of experience in an administrative role
  • Fluent in English and French

questions.texte: Analyze the advertisement and prepare for the interview.

sousQuestions.texte: Write three questions you would ask as the interviewer.
sousQuestions.reponse:
Example:
1. Could you describe your experience as an administrative assistant?
2. Would you be comfortable working with bilingual clients?
3. What would you say are your greatest organizational strengths?

sousQuestions.texte: Write three answers you would give as the candidate.
sousQuestions.reponse:
Example:
1. I have two years of experience as an administrative assistant at a law firm.
2. Yes, I am fluent in both English and French.
3. I am very organized and I pay attention to detail.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the key qualities the employer is looking for.
sousQuestions.reponse: The employer is looking for a candidate with a bachelor's degree, excellent communication and organizational skills, proficiency in Microsoft Office, two years of experience, and bilingual skills in English and French.

enonce.texte: After conducting a mock interview, write a self-assessment of your performance. Identify your strengths and areas for improvement.

questions.texte: Write a self-assessment of your mock interview performance.
questions.reponse: A well-structured self-assessment with clear analysis.

sousQuestions.texte: Example: "During the mock interview, I was able to clearly describe my experience and skills. I used polite forms like 'could' and 'would' appropriately. However, I need to improve my answers to questions about weaknesses. Next time, I will prepare better by researching common interview questions."
sousQuestions.reponse: Uses the infinitive of purpose (to improve, to prepare) and demonstrates self-reflection.
